news.title: Pepsi Earnings Beat Expectations. Why the Stock Is Dropping.
news.summary: PepsiCo  stock fell Thursday after the soft drinks and snacks maker said it had lost market share in North America, overshadowing a second-quarter earnings beat.  The maker of Lay’s, Doritos, Pepsi, and Gatorade reported an adjusted profit of $2.20 a share for the second quarter, as revenue climbed 6.4% from a year ago to $24.18 billion.
